mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future equity cash flows, directly depressing S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds +0.05 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of rate-hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions, triggering recession fears that cause immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60d returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive baseline with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output, which predicts negative short-term equity returns specifically during periods of high inflation when supply constraints dominate market sentiment.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to positive during high inflation periods, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance with t-stat below 1.5 when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, the cost of capital becomes prohibitive for highly leveraged firms, leading to earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >60% of observations above the 5.0% threshold, or Rank IC remains negative over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates a healthy labor market expansion, boosting consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Combined signal yields negative 20d returns in >55% of cases, or interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects sustained economic growth, which directly translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of GDP expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances, or signal loses predictive accuracy with Rank IC below 0.02 during recovery phases.
